description: LEFT JOIN makes right-side columns nullable even if declared NOT NULL, since unmatched rows produce NULLs

columns.name: total
columns.note: RIGHT side of LEFT JOIN: orders.total is NOT NULL in schema but becomes nullable because unmatched left rows produce NULL

name: basic_left_join
query_sql:
-- @name GetUsersWithOptionalOrders
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.id, u.name, o.total FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', bio TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly marks o.total as nullable (Option<i32>) because LEFT JOIN can produce NULL for unmatched rows
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks o.total as non-nullable (i32) because orders.total is declared NOT NULL
